metadata.name: formal_learning_theory_proof_world_model
metadata.description: Operational knowledge base encoding the metaprogram layer of the kernel's proof architecture. The primary unit is the metaprogram: a structured proof prediction with typed inputs/outputs, composition rules, and failure diagnostics. Consumable by agents for proof search routing.

sources.discovery_traces: formal-learning-theory-discovery repo (provenance for edges marked discovery_repo)

goal_profiles.description: Patterns that match classes of proof goals. An agent reads the goal type signature and matches against these patterns to select metaprograms.

strategy.output_type: GrowthFunction X C m <= polynomial(m, d)

substeps: Introduce independent ghost sample S' (double sample measure)
Exchange sup and expectation via exchangeability
Reduce to growth function counting via restriction to 2m points
Close via Hoeffding concentration on per-hypothesis gaps

metaprograms.name: VCDim finiteness from PAC learnability (contrapositive)
metaprograms.note: ~200 lines. Non-constructive direction. 15:1 asymmetry with MP_pac_chain.

metaprograms.name: Online learnability via Standard Optimal Algorithm

substeps: SOA predicts label maximizing version space Ldim
On mistake: version space restricts, Ldim strictly decreases
After d mistakes: Ldim = 0, all remaining concepts agree

metaprograms.name: Littlestone lower bound via adversary construction

strategy.input_type: OnlineLearnable X Bool C with bound M
substeps: Given shattered tree of depth n > M
Adversary inspects prediction, plays opposite label
Recurse on restricted concept class along chosen branch
After n steps: n mistakes, contradicting bound M

metaprograms.name: Gold impossibility via locking sequence

strategy.input_type: ConceptClass containing all finite subsets + infinite set
substeps: Construct monotone chain of finite sets converging to infinite target
At each stage: extend text to lock learner onto finite concept
Build adversarial text consistent with infinite target
Learner stabilizes on finite concept but text is consistent with infinite: contradiction

preconditions: C contains all finite subsets + infinite set
metaprograms.note: Paradigm-locked to Gold. Exploits enumeration structure of TextPresentation.

strategy.method: Construct concept class satisfying condition A and violating condition B
strategy.input_type: target separation: A holds but B fails
strategy.output_type: concrete class C with A(C) and not B(C)

known_instances.witness: threshold class on N: VCDim=1, LDim=infinity
finite-subset indicators on N: EX-learnable, VCDim=infinity
singleton class over analytic non-Borel A

strategy.input_type: goal with Measure.pi over infinite X

strategy.output_type: growth function bound on merged sample

metaprograms.name: Direct enumeration + union bound (finite domain)

strategy.input_type: goal with [Fintype X] or finite C

metaprograms.note: The finite-domain shortcut. Does NOT need symmetrization.

strategy.input_type: C = range of jointly measurable e : Theta x X -> Bool
strategy.output_type: bad event is projection of Borel witness graph

metaprograms.name: PAC-Bayes bound via prior-weighted Hoeffding + Jensen

strategy.input_type: prior P, hypothesis h, sample S

strategy.output_type: Gibbs error bound with KL penalty

postconditions: Gibbs error bound with KL penalty

metaprograms.name: MeasurableBatchLearner via rectangle decomposition

strategy.method: Decompose evaluation preimage into countable union of measurable rectangles
strategy.input_type: learner defined by Nat.find on countable measurable enumeration
substeps: Define IsFirstConsistent predicate (no Nat.find dependency)
Prove IsFirstConsistent events are measurable
Bridge: Nat.find h = n iff IsFirstConsistent enum S n
Decompose preimage of {true}: union_n (A_n x B_n)
Close via measurable_to_countable'

preconditions: countable enumeration enum : N -> Concept X Bool
metaprograms.note: Generalizable: any Nat.find-based selector from a countable measurable family satisfies joint measurability by the same argument.

strategy.method: Reduce interpolated class to patchEval range
strategy.input_type: interpClass(C1, C2, A) or interpClass with router

strategy.method: Apply Borel-analytic bridge to patchEval parameterization

failure_diagnostics.trigger: Agent applies symmetrization to goal with [Fintype X]
failure_diagnostics.symptom: Proof produces 2^{2m} where GrowthFunction(C, 2m) is needed
failure_diagnostics.root_cause: Symmetrization assumes infinite sample space; direct enumeration suffices on finite domains
failure_diagnostics.early_detection: Check for Fintype X or Finite X in local context

failure_diagnostics.trigger: Agent attempts direct union bound over uncountable C
failure_diagnostics.symptom: Union bound requires summing over C; undefined for uncountable sets without measure
failure_diagnostics.root_cause: Union bound works on finite/countable sets; uncountable C requires symmetrization
failure_diagnostics.early_detection: Check if C is provably finite or countable

failure_diagnostics.trigger: lintegral or integral refuses to compute at UC step
failure_diagnostics.symptom: Missing MeasurableSet or NullMeasurableSet instance for bad event
failure_diagnostics.root_cause: Bad event for uncountable C is not in the Borel sigma-algebra
failure_diagnostics.early_detection: Check for [MeasurableConceptClass X C] in context
failure_diagnostics.fix: Add [MeasurableConceptClass X C] or route to MP_borel_analytic_bridge
failure_diagnostics.provenance: kernel: discovered during symmetrization chain compilation

failure_diagnostics.trigger: uc_imp_pac fails because m0 depends on D
failure_diagnostics.symptom: Cannot extract distribution-free sample complexity function
failure_diagnostics.root_cause: UC definition uses forall D, exists m0 (pointwise) instead of exists m0, forall D (uniform)
failure_diagnostics.early_detection: Check quantifier order in HasUniformConvergence definition
failure_diagnostics.fix: Use uniform ordering: exists m0, forall D, forall m >= m0

failure_diagnostics.trigger: Characterization theorem is false under branch-wise tree shattering
failure_diagnostics.symptom: C = {const_true, const_false} gives LDim = infinity but C is 1-mistake learnable
failure_diagnostics.root_cause: Branch-wise allows independent witnesses per level; needs path-consistent witnesses
failure_diagnostics.early_detection: Check whether isShattered restricts C at recursive calls

failure_diagnostics.symptom: Proof closes in 3 lines by constructing Dm as point mass encoding target c
failure_diagnostics.root_cause: Existential Dm allows Dm to depend on c
failure_diagnostics.early_detection: Check if PACLearnable uses exists Dm (vulnerable) or Measure.pi (safe)

failure_diagnostics.metaprogram: any involving integration over exists h in C
failure_diagnostics.trigger: Target event defined by Classical.choose over uncountable set is not measurable
failure_diagnostics.symptom: lintegral refuses; MeasurableSet instance cannot be synthesized
failure_diagnostics.root_cause: Classical.choose does not produce measurable selectors for uncountable indexing sets
failure_diagnostics.early_detection: Check if goal event involves exists over uncountable set with Classical.choose
failure_diagnostics.fix: Measurable inner event pattern: find measurable E_inner subset of E_target, conclude by monotonicity
failure_diagnostics.provenance: kernel: two instances (symmetrization bad event, advice elimination GoodPair)

theorem_inventory.description: All 278 theorem/lemma declarations are instances that metaprograms operated on. The metaprogram annotations above record which programs produced which theorems. Full per-file listing available in prior commit (d0baab6).
